description: DHL Express in Europe launched a 'Trojan Horse' marketing campaign in 2012, where they tricked rival delivery drivers into delivering DHL-branded boxes to difficult locations in freezing weather. While the video went viral, the campaign sparked a backlash regarding the ethics and humiliation of the rival employees.

key_events.description: The video is released and immediately goes viral for being 'funny'.
Ethics experts and social media users begin questioning the bullying nature of the prank.
Competitor employees issue public statements condemning the stunt.
Public sentiment shifts as the 'mean-spirited' narrative takes hold over the 'creative' narrative.

calibration_notes: Useful for understanding how a brand's 'humorous' intent can be subverted by an audience's ethical scrutiny over time.
